title: Design and implement FiniteField trait for generic field arithmetic
description:
Define a two-tier trait system for generic finite field operations: 1. `FiniteField` — core trait for all field elements (const-generic and runtime-parameterized). Provides: Add, Sub, Mul, Div, Neg operators, inv(), is_zero(), is_one(), zero_like(&self), one_like(&self), characteristic() as associated type, extension_degree(). Requires Hash in supertraits. 2. `ConstField: FiniteField + Copy` — subtrait for compile-time-known fields. Adds: zero() -> Self, one() -> Self, order() -> u64. Only for const-generic types (Fp

, Gf2_8, etc.), not for runtime-parameterized types (Gf2mElement). 3. `FiniteFieldExt` — blanket-implemented extension trait with default impls for square(), pow(), frobenius(). Design for monomorphization so the compiler can specialize to zero-cost machine code for const-generic types. See dev/plans/gfpn_groundwork_analysis.md section 4.1 for full specification.
